item.title: Dissident who fled China by boat arrives in Canada, his friend says
item.description: Dong Guangping landed in Toronto following an Air Canada flight on Friday, his friend said.

item.title: Saturday Sessions: Kevin Morby performs "Javelin"
item.description: Kansas City-based singer-songwriter Kevin Morby has been likened to Bob Dylan and Tom Petty, with his rich, soulful lyrics across eight studio albums. His latest album, "Little Wide Open" was produced by The National's Aaron Dessner. Here's Kevin Morby performing "Javelin."

item.title: Saturday Sessions: Kevin Morby performs "Badlands"
item.description: Kansas City-based singer-songwriter Kevin Morby has been likened to Bob Dylan and Tom Petty, with his rich, soulful lyrics across eight studio albums. His latest album, "Little Wide Open" was produced by The National's Aaron Dessner. Here's Kevin Morby performing "Badlands."

item.title: Saturday Sessions: Kevin Morby performs "100,000"
item.description: Kansas City-based singer-songwriter Kevin Morby has been likened to Bob Dylan and Tom Petty, with his rich, soulful lyrics across eight studio albums. His latest album, "Little Wide Open" was produced by The National's Aaron Dessner. Here's Kevin Morby performing "100,000."

item.title: Danny McBride on why he chose to write about masculinity in his new book
item.description: Danny McBride's new book, "Thrilling Tales of Modern Men," explores themes of masculinity through funny and dark short stories.

item.title: U.S. strikes Iran after Strait of Hormuz attack
item.description: The U.S. launched retaliatory strikes on Iran after Iranian forces hit a vessel in the Strait of Hormuz on Thursday. President Trump called the attack a "foolish violation" of the ceasefire between nations.

item.title: Fireworks sales soar ahead of Fourth of July
item.description: Backyard fireworks sales in the U.S. have skyrocketed this year. Meanwhile, gas and ground beef prices remain high.

item.title: FAA investigates viral low-flying cargo plane at Houston airport
item.description: The FAA says it's investigating a Qatar Airways cargo jet that could be seen in viral videos flying extremely low to the ground near Texas airports.

item.title: Lebanese civilians divided over Israel-Hezbollah conflict
item.description: "CBS Saturday Morning" goes inside Lebanon to learn about the deep divisions surrounding the clashes between Israel and Hezbollah.

item.title: Iranian drones target Bahrain after U.S. strikes Iran
item.description: The U.S. carried out retaliatory strikes against Iran on Friday after Iranian forces hit a cargo vessel in the Strait of Hormuz a day earlier.

item.title: Venezuelans desperatey search for earthquake survivors: "As long as it takes"
item.description: Venezuelans are desperately searching for survivors after two deadly back-to-back earthquakes rocked the South American country three days ago.

item.title: Eye Opener: U.S. launches retaliatory strikes on Iran
item.description: The U.S. launched retaliatory strikes on Iran Friday. Meanwhile, Europeans are battling a deadly heat wave. All that and all that matters in today's Eye Opener.

item.title: Search for earthquake survivors in Venezuela stretches into 3rd day
item.description: Venezuelans have taken the search for missing loved ones into their own hands, citing a scarcity of government rescuers.

item.title: Mom gets answers after fighting for diagnosis: "Something's wrong"
item.description: Michelle Williams struggled with high blood pressure and swelling for years before she was finally diagnosed with an unusual condition.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: The Uplift: The American Dream
item.description: We celebrate the United States ahead of its 250th anniversary with stories about American heroes and pastimes. The founder of e.l.f. Cosmetics shares how he made a massive life change to become a Catholic priest.

item.title: Letlow, Fleming face off for Cassidy's seat in Louisiana GOP Senate runoff
item.description: Louisianans are voting Saturday in the state's Republican Senate runoff, as two candidates vie to replace Sen. Bill Cassidy, who did not receive enough votes in the primary to advance.

item.title: Buttigieg targeted by fake report to child protective services
item.description: Michigan State Police said law enforcement and Child Protective Services confirmed a report against Pete Buttigieg was unsubstantiated and false.

item.title: Utah declares emergency, limits fireworks as crews battle largest U.S. wildfire
item.description: Utah is restricting fireworks as the largest wildfire in the nation grows, fueled by dry conditions and gusting winds.

item.title: Exonerated man's widow calls settlement in yogurt shop murders "blood money"
item.description: The city of Austin agreed to a multimillion-dollar settlement to be split among four men — including to the widow and daughter of Maurice Pierce — who were wrongfully accused of murdering four teenage girls in a Texas yogurt shop.

item.title: U.S. strikes targets in Iran after drone attack on cargo ship
item.description: The U.S. military says it hit Iranian targets over Iran's drone attack on a commercial vessel in the Strait of Hormuz, marking the first American strikes on Iran since the two countries formally agreed to extend a ceasefire last week.

item.title: Race to find survivors as Venezuela's earthquake death toll soars
item.description: The twin earthquakes that hit Venezuela killed more than 900 people, and that toll is likely to keep rising as frantic rescue and recovery operations ramp up.

item.title: Nurse who went above and beyond gets special send-off: "You're an angel"
item.description: Wynola Wayne received a special retirement send-off after 58 years as a nurse. One former patient, Marco Houpe, said, "If it wasn't for her then, I wouldn't be here today."

item.title: Video shows Boeing 777 making very low pass over Texas airfield
item.description: Data from FlightRadar24 showed the plane was no more than 25 feet above the ground during the low pass as it approached the Horseshoe Bay Resort Jet Center airport.

item.title: U.S. loses to Turkey in 2026 World Cup, moves to knockout round
item.description: The U.S. Men's National Soccer Team suffered its first loss of the 2026 World Cup Thursday night, but will still advance to the knockout round to face Bosnia and Herzegovina on Wednesday. Nicole Valdes has more.

item.title: 6/26: CBS Evening News
item.description: Desperate searches are underway for earthquake survivors in Venezuela; a large jet makes a frighteningly low flyover.

item.title: 6/26: The Takeout with Major Garrett
item.description: Leon Black testifies before a House panel on the Epstein files; U.S. conducts retaliatory strikes against Iran.

item.title: Judge in Charlie Kirk murder case finds prosecutor in contempt
item.description: The defense team for Tyler Robinson asked that the death penalty be taken off the table following public comments by prosecutors.

item.title: "Regime Change" authors on Trump's "information bubble" and use of power
item.description: In "Regime Change, Inside the Imperial Presidency of Donald Trump," Maggie Haberman and Jonathan Swan describe his fundamentally different approach to running the country.

item.title: Heat waves push more Europeans to embrace air conditioning
item.description: Only 20% European homes have AC, compared to 90% in the U.S., but as the climate changes, that vast gulf may be set to shrink.
